articles.title: Khamenei lies in state in Tehran as Iran begins week of mass funeral ceremonies
articles.summary: The body of slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, killed in a joint US-Israeli strike on February 28, arrived at Tehran's Grand Mosalla to lie in state for three days as Iran opened a week of funeral ceremonies running July 4-9 across Tehran, Qom and Mashhad in Iran and Najaf and Karbala in Iraq. Officials expect 15-20 million mourners, which would make it the largest state funeral in Iran's history; dozens of foreign delegations are attending, and President Pezeshkian and Speaker Ghalibaf greeted dignitaries at the hall. The main Tehran procession is set for July 6 and burial for July 9 at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad.

articles.title: Foreign officials arrive for Khamenei funeral as IRGC general emerges from hiding and warns Israel, US against strikes
articles.summary: As foreign delegations arrived in Tehran for the funeral, a senior Iranian general warned Israel and the US against any strikes during the ceremonies. Gen. Ahmad Vahidi, who leads the IRGC's paramilitary forces, emerged from months in hiding, appearing in photos at a funeral-planning meeting and beside Khamenei's casket.

articles.title: Trump says Iran has agreed to 'just about everything' US needs as talks pause for funeral
articles.summary: President Trump told CNBC that Iran has 'agreed to just about everything we need' following the latest round of indirect talks in Doha. Negotiations are paused during the week of Khamenei funeral ceremonies, with the next round expected on July 18, according to Al Arabiya sources.

articles.title: Netanyahu and Trump speak by phone, agree to meet in US soon
articles.summary: PM Netanyahu and President Trump spoke by phone and agreed to meet in the US in the near future, the Prime Minister's Office said. Netanyahu congratulated Trump on the 250th anniversary of America's founding; the call comes amid US-Iran negotiations and follow-through on the Lebanon framework agreement.

articles.title: Mourners bury Damascus cafe blast victims as ministry says shrapnel-packed device caused attack
articles.summary: Damascus buried victims of Thursday's bombing at a cafe near the capital's main judicial complex as officials promised justice; reported tolls rose to as many as 9-10 dead and about 20 wounded. Syria's interior ministry said preliminary findings point to a roughly one-kilogram explosive device packed with metal shrapnel; SANA reported a further arrest, no group has claimed the attack, and investigators are examining sleeper cells seeking to destabilize the post-Assad government. The health minister visited the wounded and pledged full medical care.

articles.title: Lebanon's Aoun defends trilateral agreement, says it 'does not legitimize' Israeli military presence
articles.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun defended the framework agreement reached in Washington, insisting it 'does not legitimize the continuation of the Israeli occupation in Lebanon' and that the shared objective is 'to secure Israel's withdrawal.'

articles.title: Israeli drone strikes injure 2 in southern Lebanon despite framework agreement
articles.summary: Two people were injured Friday in Israeli drone strikes on a vehicle in Lebanon's southern Tyre district, in what Lebanese authorities described as another violation of the framework agreement between Beirut and Tel Aviv. Separately, Israeli media reported an IDF reservist was severely wounded in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Over 640,000 displaced Lebanese return home amid ceasefire, IOM says
articles.summary: More than 640,000 displaced people in Lebanon have returned home as clashes between Hezbollah and Israel wind down following the deal to end the Middle East war, according to the International Organization for Migration.

articles.title: UN human rights investigators begin 'first of its kind' mission in Lebanon
articles.summary: A team of UN human rights investigators is on the ground in Lebanon collecting evidence on violations committed by all parties to the conflict since March 2, after Beirut granted guarantees for the mission's deployment.

articles.title: France and Italy preparing new international force for southern Lebanon after UNIFIL
articles.summary: France's foreign ministry said preparations are under way with Italy to form a new international coalition force to deploy in southern Lebanon once the mandate of the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) ends.

articles.title: Israel approves 13 new settlements as Palestinians warn of Jerusalem's further isolation
articles.summary: Israel's Security Cabinet approved a plan to establish 13 new settlements in the central occupied West Bank. Palestinian officials warned the move would further fragment the territory and isolate East Jerusalem from its Palestinian surroundings.

articles.title: IDF and Shin Bet kill Hamas commander who abducted Cpt. Daniel Perez and held hostages
articles.summary: The IDF and Shin Bet said they killed Muhammad Na'im Jandiya, head of military security for Hamas's Shajaiyah Battalion, who participated in the October 7 abduction of tank officer Cpt. Daniel Perez and held hostages in Gaza.

articles.title: Settler attack injures several Palestinians near Hebron
articles.summary: Several Palestinians were injured in an attack by Israeli settlers near Hebron in the occupied West Bank, amid a wider pattern of settler violence; the Israeli army also dispersed a protest against a settlement outpost near Bethlehem, injuring and arresting several Palestinians.

articles.title: In the West Bank, Israeli settlers take over a Palestinian family's dream home
articles.summary: Feature on Mohammad Salameh, a Palestinian building a home for his family in the occupied West Bank that was seized by a group of Israeli settlers before construction was complete, illustrating accelerating settler takeovers of Palestinian property.

articles.title: China calls for 'proper settlement' to end disruptions in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: China called for a 'proper settlement' to end disruptions to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz as the US and Iran held indirect talks in Qatar, with Washington having told Tehran it rejects any change to the strait's status quo.

articles.title: Palestinian Foreign Ministry denies asking South Africa to withdraw ICJ case against Israel
articles.summary: The Palestinian Ministry of Foreign Affairs categorically denied claims that it requested South Africa withdraw its genocide case against Israel before the International Court of Justice.

articles.title: Israeli army confirms authenticity of image showing Palestinian detainee abused in Gaza
articles.summary: The Israeli army confirmed the authenticity of a widely circulated image showing a Palestinian detainee being abused by Israeli forces in the Gaza Strip, saying the incident is under review.

articles.title: 'Extravagant ceremony for a dictator': Some Iranians say they will avoid Khamenei's funeral
articles.summary: While authorities expect mass turnout, some Iranians told The Times of Israel they will stay away from the week of funeral ceremonies, describing them as an extravagant send-off for a leader they blame for repression and the war's devastation.

articles.title: Exclusive: ICC prosecutors shelved RSF arrest warrant as Sudan atrocities mounted
articles.summary: Middle East Eye reports the ICC prosecutor's office has not applied for a single arrest warrant over crimes in Sudan's Darfur region since the war began in April 2023, shelving a prepared RSF warrant even as atrocities mounted.

articles.title: Outrage after Turkish stand-up comedian arrested for 'insulting' Erdogan
articles.summary: Turkish authorities arrested a popular stand-up comedian accused of insulting President Erdogan and 'insulting religious values', drawing widespread criticism over freedom of expression.
